messages.content:
мне нужно чтобы ты мне написал выступление как в этом примере: 
This won’t take more than 4 minutes. There will be time for questions after my presentation. 
First of all, let me thank you all for coming here today. 
Let me introduce myself. I'm <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, student of NTU "KhPI"
The topic of my presentation is «Ecological problems in the world».
I’ve divided my presentation into three parts. Today's topic is of particular interest to those of you who want to know the causes of environmental pollution.
I will tell you about the 3 main   by humanity.
On the first slide you can see water pollution. It is the contamination of water sources by substances which make the water unusable for drinking, cooking, cleaning, swimming, and other activities. Pollutants include chemicals, trash, bacteria, and parasites. All forms of pollution eventually make their way to water.
 	The next slide shows air pollution. It is contamination of the indoor or outdoor environment by any chemical, physical or biological agent that modifies the natural characteristics of the atmosphere. Household combustion devices, motor vehicles, industrial facilities and forest fires are common sources of air pollution.
	On the last slide present ozone layer. The main cause of ozone depletion and the ozone hole is manufactured chemicals, especially manufactured halocarbon refrigerants, solvents, propellants, and foam-blowing agents, CFCs.
That’s all I wanted to say about ecological problems in the world. 
Thanks for your attention. Any questions?

тема выступления «Ecological problems in the world». мне нужно что бы ты обьязательно использовал вот это: Фразы, необходимые для доклада по теме презентации 
USEFUL PHRASES AND VOCABULARY 
 
Introduction 
Timing 
My presentation will take about 5 minutes.  
This won’t take more than… 
Handouts 
Don’t worry about taking notes. I’ve put all the important statistics on a handout for you.  
I can email the Power Point presentation to anybody who wants it.  
Questions  
There will be time for questions after my presentation. 
If you have any questions, feel free to interrupt me at any time. Feel free to ask questions at any time during my talk. 
Welcoming the audience 
Good morning / afternoon, ladies and gentlemen. 
Hello / Hi everyone. 
First of all, let me thank you all for coming here today. 
I’m happy / delighted that so many of you could make it today. 
Introducing yourself  
Let me introduce myself. I’m (Ann Brown) from …. 
For those of you who don’t know me, my name is…. 
Giving your position, function, department, company 
I’m here in my function as the head of… 
I’m the project manager in charge of… 
I’m a student of… 
Introducing your topic 
What I’d like to present to you today is… 
I’m here today to present… 
Today’s topic is… 
The subject / topic of my presentation is… 
Today I’m going to talk about… 
Structuring  
I’ve divided my presentation into three (main) parts. 
In my presentation I’ll focus on three major issues.  
Sequencing 
Point one deals with…  point two…  point three… 
First I’ll be looking at… . Second … . And third… . 
I’ll begin / start off by… . then I’ll move on to … 
Then / next / after that… 
Saying why your topic is relevant for your audience 
Today’s topic is of particular interest to those of you / us who… 
My talk is particularly relevant to those of us who… 
By the end of this talk you will be familiar with… 
Stating your purpose 
The purpose / objective / aim of this presentation is to… 
Our goal is to determine how / the best way to… 
What I want to show you is… 
My objective is to… 
I’d like to update you on / inform you about… 
 
Effective openings 
Rhetorical questions  
Is market research important for brand development? 
Do we really need quality assurance? 
Interesting facts 
According to an article I read recently … 
Did you know that...? 
I’d like to share an amazing fact / figure with you. 
Stories and anecdotes 
I remember when I attended a meeting in Paris…. 
At a conference in Madrid, I was once asked the following question… 
Let me tell you what happened to me… 
Problem to think about  
Suppose you wanted to… How would you go about it?  
Imagine you had to… What would be your first step? 
 
The middle / main part 
 
Saying what is coming  
In this            of my presentation I’d like to talk about… 
So, let me first give you a brief overview… 
Indicating the end of a section 
This brings me to the end of my first point 
That’s all I wanted to say about… 
Summarizing a point  
Let me briefly summarize the main issues. 
I’d like to summarize what I’ve said so far… 
Moving to the next point 
This brings directly to my next point. 
This brings us to the next question. 
Let’s now take a look at… 
Going back 
As I said / mentioned earlier… 
Let me come back to what I said before… 
Let’s go back to what we were discussing earlier. 
As I’ve already explained… 
Adding ideas 
Moreover / furthermore, there are other interesting facts we should look at. 
Talking about (difficult) issues 
I think we first need to identify the problem. 
Of course we’ll have to clarify a few points before we start. 
We will have to deal with the problem of increasing prices. 
If we don’t solve this problem now, we’ll get / run into serious trouble soon. 
Rhetorical questions 
What conclusion can we draw from this? 
So, what does this mean? 
So, just how good are the results? 
So, how are we going to deal with this increase?
Describing visuals 
 
Introducing a visual 
Let’s now look at the next slide which shows… 
The chart on the following slide shows… 
According to this graph, our net profit has doubled. 
You can see the test results in this table. 
As you can see here… 
Explaining a visual 
First, let me quickly explain the graph. 
You can see that different colours have been used to indicate… 
The biggest segment indicates… 
Highlighting information 
I’d like to stress / highlight / emphasize the following point(s). 
I’d like to start by drawing your attention to… 
I’ll think you will be surprised to see that… 
I’d like you to focus your attention on… 
What’s really important here is… 
Explaining purpose 
We introduced this method to increase flexibility. 
The purpose of this step is to expand to foreign markets. 
Our aim was to… 
Explaining cause and effect 
The unexpected drop was caused by… 
As a consequence / Consequently, sales went up significantly. 
As a result… 
Our new strategy has led to an increase of 10 % 
 
Conclusion 
Indicating the end of your talk 
Well, this brings me to the end of my presentation. 
Ok, I think that’s everything I wanted to say about… 
Finally, I’d like to highlight one key issue. 
Summarizing points 
Before I stop, let me go over the key issues again. 
I’d like to run through my main points again… 
To conclude / in conclusion, I’d like to… 
Making recommendations 
We’d suggest… 
In my opinion, we should… 
Based on the figures we have, I’m quite certain that… 
Inviting questions 
Are there any questions? 
And now I’ll be happy to answer any questions you may have. 
Effective conclusions 
Quoting a well-known person  
As… once said,…  
To quote a well-known businessman,… 
To put it in the words of… 
Referring back to the beginning 
Remember what I said at the beginning of my talk today? 
Let me just go back to the story I told you earlier. 
 
Dealing with questions 
 
Clarifying questions 
I’m afraid I didn’t (quite) catch that. 
I’m sorry, could you repeat your question, please? 
So, if I understood you correctly, you would like to know whether… 
Avoiding giving an answer 
If you don’t mind, could we discuss that on another occasion?  
I’m afraid that’s not really what we’re discussing today. 
Well, actually I’d prefer not to discuss that today. 
Admitting you don’t know 
I’m afraid I’m not in a position to answer that question at the moment. 
I’m afraid I don’t know the answer to your question, but I’ll try to find out for you. 
Sorry, that’s not my field. But I’m sure Peter Bott from Sales could answer your question. 
 
Useful words (in context) 
 
to clarify    Before we go on, let me clarify one point. 
to focus on   We need to focus on customer service. 
to highlight   Let me highlight the following points. 
to illustrate   This chart illustrates our success story. 
to indicate   The figures on the left indicate sales in France. 
to lead to    This leads to my next point. 
to mention   As I mentioned earlier, our staff is well qualified.  
to move on to   Let’s now move on to the next question. 
to note    Please note that prices rose slightly. 
to notice   You’ll notice a sharp drop in August. 
to pass on   Here are the handouts. Please take one and pass them on. 
to rise    House prices rose by 5% last year. 
to solve   How can we solve this problem? 
to summarize  Before I go on, let me summarize the key issues. 
to update   I’d like to update you on the project status.

My presentation will take about 5 minutes, and I'd be glad to address any questions you may have at the end. If you'd like to keep a record of this talk, don’t worry about taking notes—I’ve compiled all the crucial statistics on a handout for you, and I can also email the PowerPoint presentation to anyone interested.

Good morning, ladies and gentlemen. First of all, let me thank you all for coming here today. Your presence is greatly appreciated. 

For those of you who don't know me, my name is <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>. I’m here today as a dedicated student of NTU "KhPI" with a profound concern for our planet.

The subject of my presentation is "Ecological problems in the world"—a pressing issue that affects each and every one of us. Given its significance, this topic is particularly relevant to individuals who strive to comprehend the roots of environmental degradation and wish to contribute to its resolution.

Throughout this presentation, I've organized my thoughts into three distinct sections, each focusing on a major issue inflicted by human activity upon the environment.

As we proceed to the first slide, you'll notice it centers on water pollution. This is the contamination of water bodies by detrimental substances that negate their utility across various human activities, including drinking, cooking, cleaning, and swimming. These pollutants range from chemicals and garbage to harmful microorganisms.

Moving on, the second slide illustrates the pervasive problems of air pollution. This is the alteration of indoor or outdoor air quality by chemical, physical, or biological agents, leading to an environment that can harm human health and other living beings. Common contributors to air pollution include household combustion devices, motor vehicles, industrial facilities, and uncontrolled forest fires.

Arriving at the final slide, we address the critical issue of ozone layer depletion. The principal culprits here are certain manufactured chemicals, particularly CFCs, which are used across various domains, from refrigeration and propellants to solvents and foam-blowing agents.

To conclude, the ecological challenges we face globally are substantial, but understanding them is the first step towards mitigation and, ultimately, resolutions. 

Well, this brings me to the end of my presentation. I hope it has been informative and insightful. Before I invite any questions I would like to remind you that our actions today shape our environment of tomorrow.

Are there any questions?

напиши мне сначала Introduction. потом Handouts. потом Questions. потом Welcoming the audience. потом Introducing yourself. потом Introducing topic. потом Structuring, 4 parts. потом Sequencing. потом Saying why topic is relevant for your audience. потом Stating your purpose. потом Effective openings. потом The middle / main part, Saying what is coming. потом Summarizing a point. потом Moving to the next point. потом Moving to the next point. потом Going back. потом Adding ideas. потом Rhetorical questions. потом Introducing a visual. потом Explaining a visual. потом Highlighting information. потом Explaining purpose. потом Explaining cause and effect. потом Conclusion. потом Summarizing points. потом Making recommendations. потом Inviting questions. потом Effective conclusions. также добавь в текст обьязательно Useful words (in context):
to clarify 
to focus on 
to highlight 
to illustrate 
to indicate 
to lead to 
to mention 
to move on to 
to note 
to notice 
to pass on 
to rise 
to solve 
to summarize 
to update

**Introduction**  
Good morning, ladies and gentlemen, and thank you for joining me today. My presentation will take about 5 minutes. I’ll be discussing a matter of critical importance that touches all our lives.

**Handouts**  
Before we get started, please note that I've prepared handouts so you can follow along with the main points and statistics. These will be passed around. Additionally, for those who find it more convenient, I can email the PowerPoint presentation after our meeting.

**Questions**  
If at any point during my talk you have questions, feel free to ask. There will also be time after the presentation to address any additional questions you may have.

**Welcoming the Audience**  
First of all, let me express my gratitude for your presence. I’m happy to see so many of you could make it today.

**Introducing Yourself**  
For those of you who don’t know me, my name is <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, and I am a student of NTU "KhPI" with a keen interest in environmental studies.

**Introducing Your Topic**  
The topic I am here to present today is "Ecological problems in the world." It is a subject that, I believe, warrants our immediate attention and collective action.

**Structuring**  
I’ve structured my presentation into four main parts to provide a comprehensive view of the complexities involved in this subject.

**Sequencing**  
First, I will clarify the scope of global pollution. Then I’ll focus on highlighting specific types of pollution that we frequently encounter. Following that, I’ll discuss the consequences of these ecological challenges on our health and environment. Lastly, I will talk about potential solutions and strategies to mitigate these problems.

**Saying Why Your Topic Is Relevant for Your Audience**  
Today's topic is of particular interest to us as it directly impacts the quality of life and the sustainability of the environments in which we live and work.

**Stating Your Purpose**  
The objective of my presentation is to raise awareness and to update you on the rising environmental issues that we face globally.

**Effective Openings**  
Did you know that the air we breathe and the water we use are becoming more polluted by the day, threatening not just humans but all forms of life on our planet? 

**The Middle / Main Part**  
Moving on to the main part of my presentation, I will now illustrate the various dimensions of global ecological problems.

**Summarizing a Point**  
To summarize what we've discussed so far, water and air pollution are significant challenges that have been escalating in intensity due to human activity.

**Moving to the Next Point**  
This naturally leads us to the next part of the discussion: identifying the most pressing ecological problems that need our attention.

**Going Back**  
As mentioned at the beginning, the central theme here is pollution. Let's go back to that and note how it's directly linked to several other ecological concerns.

**Adding Ideas**  
Furthermore, it's crucial to consider the domino effect of these environmental issues, indicative of a larger ecological crisis.

**Rhetorical Questions**  
So, just how profound is the impact of these ecological problems? And what can we actually do to address them?

**Introducing a Visual**  
Let me move on to the next slide, which shows a comprehensive view of global pollution statistics and trends over the past decade.

**Explaining a Visual**  
As you can see here, the figures on the right indicate a rise in emissions, highlighting the growing scale of the problem.

**Highlighting Information**  
I'd like to emphasize the stark increase in plastic pollution, as depicted by the red areas on the graph.

**Explaining Purpose**  
Our aim here is to identify these trends in order to formulate effective strategies for mitigation.

**Explaining Cause and Effect**  
The spike in industrial activities, as you'll notice, has led to an increase in the contaminants released into our ecosystems.

**Conclusion**  
This brings me to the end of my presentation. I hope this discussion has provided a clearer perspective on the pervasive ecological problems we face.

**Summarizing Points**  
Before I conclude, let me quickly summarize the key points: first, the undeniable importance of tackling pollution; and second, our collective responsibility in driving ecological change.

**Making Recommendations**  
Based on what we've learned, I would suggest a more stringent approach to waste management and stronger environmental regulations.

**Inviting Questions**  
Now, I invite you to ask any questions. Your insights and queries are most welcome.

**Effective Conclusions**  
In closing, I'd like to quote the words of the renowned conservationist Jane Goodall: "What you do makes a difference, and you have to decide what kind of difference you want to make." 

Let's make that difference together. Thank you for your attention.
добавь мне 4 абзаца по загрезнению окружающей среды. со всем тем, что я тебе писала.

**Water Pollution**  
Переходя к первому пункту, давайте сосредоточимся на загрязнении воды. Это одна из наиболее острых проблем, с которой мы сталкиваемся. Загрязнение воды включает в себя не только промышленные отходы, но и бытовой мусор, сельскохозяйственные удобрения, которые приводят к эвтрофикации и гибели водных экосистем. Позвольте мне подчеркнуть, что чистая вода – это ресурс, поддержание качества которого имеет решающее значение для нашего здоровья и будущего.

**Air Pollution**  
Следующий пункт, на который я хочу обратить ваше внимание – это загрязнение воздуха. В этом контексте я хотел бы прояснить, что под загрязнением воздуха понимается не только выход отработанных газов автомобилей, но и выбросы промышленных предприятий, сжигание твердых отходов и даже выбросы вулканов. Эти факторы влияют на качество воздуха, который мы вдыхаем, и при этом могут привести к увеличению случаев респираторных заболеваний в обществе.

**Soil Pollution**  
Теперь давайте перейдем к вопросу загрязнения почвы. Здесь следует отметить, что почва становится контейнером для множества химических веществ, отходов производства и сельского хозяйства, что снижает ее плодородие и может привести к накоплению токсичных веществ в пищевых продуктах. Как следствие, это загрязнение может повлечь за собой долгосрочные изменения в экосистемах и угрожать продовольственной безопасности.

**Noise Pollution**  
Наконец, позвольте мне обозначить проблему шумового загрязнения. Возможно, мы не всегда уделяем ей должное внимание, но постоянный шум от транспорта, строительных площадок и промышленных объектов может серьезно повлиять на наше здоровье, вызывая стресс и снижая качество жизни. Поэтому необходимо учитывать и такой аспект нашего влияния на окружающую среду и стремиться к его минимизации.

Каждый из этих пунктов является важным компонентом разговора о экологических проблемах, с которыми мы сталкиваемся в мире. Переходя к следующему визуальному слайду, вы заметите подробные графики и диаграммы, которые иллюстрируют озвученные мною факты и цифры.
замени Noise Pollution на чтото другое

**Deforestation**  
На заключительном этапе нашего обсуждения сфокусируемся на обезлесении. Этот аспект экологических проблем, хоть и может показаться удаленным от промышленных выбросов, но весьма важен для обсуждения. Обезлесение приводит не только к потере мест обитания для множества видов животных и растений, но и нарушает карбоновый баланс планеты, усугубляя парниковый эффект. Хочу подчеркнуть, что сохранение лесов критично для поддержания биоразнообразия и климатической стабильности Земли.

Этот вопрос напрямую связан с углеродным следом, который мы оставляем, и требует от нас разработки решений для сохранения лесных экосистем. Теперь, когда мы перейдем к следующему слайду, вы увидите доходчивые примеры и последствия обезлесения, а также действия, которые мы можем предпринять для борьбы с этим процессом.

добавь вот это к этому тексту: Water Pollution
Moving on to the first point, let's focus on water pollution. This is one of the most pressing problems we face. Water pollution includes not only industrial waste, but also household rubbish, agricultural fertilisers that lead to eutrophication and death of aquatic ecosystems. Let me emphasise that clean water is a resource whose quality maintenance is critical to our health and future.

Air Pollution
The next point I want to draw your attention to is air pollution. In this context, I would like to clarify that air pollution does not only refer to the exhaust emissions from cars, but also to industrial emissions, solid waste incineration and even volcanic emissions. These factors affect the quality of the air we breathe in and in doing so can lead to an increase in the incidence of respiratory diseases in society.

Soil Pollution
Now let us move on to the issue of soil pollution. Here it is worth noting that soil becomes a container for a multitude of chemicals, industrial and agricultural waste, which reduces its fertility and can lead to the accumulation of toxic substances in food. As a consequence, this pollution can cause long-term changes in ecosystems and threaten food security.

Deforestation
For the final stage of our discussion, let us focus on deforestation. This aspect of environmental problems, although it may seem remote from industrial emissions, is very important to discuss. Deforestation not only results in the loss of habitat for a multitude of animal and plant species, but also disrupts the carbon balance of the planet, exacerbating the greenhouse effect. I would like to emphasise that preserving forests is critical to maintaining biodiversity and the Earth's climate stability.

**Effective Openings**  
Is the degradation of our natural environment inevitable, or can we take decisive action to turn the tide? Did you know that every minute, forests the size of 20 football fields are cut down? At a conference in Madrid, I was once asked how environmental policies could realistically reduce pollution. Let me tell you, that question has stayed with me and has been a driving force behind my research. Today, we have a problem to think about: If we had to redesign our engagement with the environment from scratch, what would be our first step?

**The Middle / Main Part**

**Water Pollution**  
In this part of my presentation, I'd like to talk about water pollution. This pressing problem is more than industrial waste; it's about how we, as a society, handle substances that lead to the eutrophication of our waters and the death of aquatic ecosystems. Before moving on, let me summarise the main issues: water pollution threatens not just the vitality of our ecosystems, but the very quality of the water we depend on for our health and future.

**Air Pollution**  
This brings me directly to my next point: air pollution. Let's clarify that the problem extends beyond car exhaust. It encompasses the industrial emissions that cloud our skies, the incineration of solid waste, and the volcanic activities that darken our horizons. What conclusion can we draw from this? That the quality of the air we breathe is under siege, and the rise in respiratory diseases is a clear sign of this increasing threat.

**Soil Pollution**  
Let’s now take a look at soil pollution. As I mentioned earlier, soil is becoming a container for harmful substances, with a profound impact on its fertility and the safety of the foods it yields. If we don’t solve this problem now, we’ll run into serious trouble soon, with long-term changes to our ecosystems and a looming threat to our food security.

**Deforestation**  
Moving on to deforestation, let me come back to what I said before about pollution and expand on it. Deforestation is a critical issue that often slips under the radar when discussing industrial emissions. Moreover, I'd like to highlight the immense role that forests play in maintaining the carbon balance and the global climate system. To put it in the words of a conservationist I met in Paris, "To destroy a forest is to silence the voices of nature that sustain us."

**Conclusion**  
To illustrate and conclude, the visuals we’ve looked at today indicate a clear and present danger to our ecological systems. We've seen how pollution affects our water, air, and soil, and how deforestation further aggravates the global climate crisis. So, how are we going to deal with this increase in environmental degradation? I'd like to offer some recommendations and invite your thoughts on this critical subject. Before we wrap up, I’ll be happy to answer any questions you might have. Remember what I said at the beginning? Our actions today can either contribute to the problem or the solution. The choice is ours. Thank you.

My presentation will take about 5 minutes. There will be time for questions after my presentation. 

Good morning, ladies and gentlemen. First of all, let me thank you all for coming here today.

For those of you who don’t know me, my name is ......, and I am a student of NTU “KhPI”.

I’m here today to present “Ecological problems in the world.” 

In my presentation I’ll focus on four major issues. Today’s topic is of particular interest to us as it directly impacts the quality of life and the sustainability of the environments in which we live and work.

I will tell you about the 4 main by humanity.

Is the degradation of our natural environment inevitable, or can we take decisive action to turn the tide? Did you know that every minute, forests the size of 20 football fields are cut down? 

Water Pollution
In this part of my presentation, I’d like to talk about water pollution. This pressing problem is more than industrial waste; it’s about how we, as a society, handle substances that lead to the eutrophication of our waters and the death of aquatic ecosystems. Before moving on, let me summarise the main issues: water pollution threatens not just the vitality of our ecosystems, but the very quality of the water we depend on for our health and future.

Air Pollution
This brings me directly to my next point: air pollution. Let’s clarify that the problem extends beyond car exhaust. It encompasses the industrial emissions that cloud our skies, the incineration of solid waste, and the volcanic activities that darken our horizons. What conclusion can we draw from this? That the quality of the air we breathe is under siege, and the rise in respiratory diseases is a clear sign of this increasing threat.

Soil Pollution
Let’s now take a look at soil pollution. As I mentioned earlier, soil is becoming a container for harmful substances, with a profound impact on its fertility and the safety of the foods it yields. If we don’t solve this problem now, we’ll run into serious trouble soon, with long-term changes to our ecosystems and a looming threat to our food security.

Deforestation
Moving on to deforestation, let me come back to what I said before about pollution and expand on it. Deforestation is a critical issue that often slips under the radar when discussing industrial emissions. Moreover, I’d like to highlight the immense role that forests play in maintaining the carbon balance and the global climate system. To put it in the words of a conservationist I met in Paris, “To destroy a forest is to silence the voices of nature that sustain us.”

In conclusion, I’d like to say that, the points we’ve looked at today indicate a clear and present danger to our ecological systems. We’ve seen how pollution affects our water, air, and soil, and how deforestation further aggravates the global climate crisis. 
Thanks for your attention. And now I’ll be happy to answer any questions you may have.



напиши мне текст на слайды по этому тексту

**Slide 1: Introduction**

In this part of my presentation, I’d like to talk about water pollution. This pressing problem is more than industrial waste; it’s about how we, as a society, handle substances that lead to the eutrophication of our waters and the death of aquatic ecosystems. Before moving on, let me summarise the main issues: water pollution threatens not just the vitality of our ecosystems, but the very quality of the water we depend on for our health and future.
Air Pollution
This brings me directly to my next point: air pollution. Let’s clarify that the problem extends beyond car exhaust. It encompasses the industrial emissions that cloud our skies, the incineration of solid waste, and the volcanic activities that darken our horizons. What conclusion can we draw from this? That the quality of the air we breathe is under siege, and the rise in respiratory diseases is a clear sign of this increasing threat.
Soil Pollution
Let’s now take a look at soil pollution. As I mentioned earlier, soil is becoming a container for harmful substances, with a profound impact on its fertility and the safety of the foods it yields. If we don’t solve this problem now, we’ll run into serious trouble soon, with long-term changes to our ecosystems and a looming threat to our food security.
Deforestation
Moving on to deforestation, let me come back to what I said before about pollution and expand on it. Deforestation is a critical issue that often slips under the radar when discussing industrial emissions. Moreover, I’d like to highlight the immense role that forests play in maintaining the carbon balance and the global climate system. To put it in the words of a conservationist I met in Paris, “To destroy a forest is to silence the voices of nature that sustain us.”
